original_hypothesis.title: De-whitelisting USDC Version Permanently Locks All Deposited Funds of That Version
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ASDUSDC.withdraw() requires whitelistedUSDCVersions[_usdcVersion] to be true. If the owner de-whitelists a USDC version via updateWhitelist(version, false), all depositors of that version lose access to their USDC. Their asdUSDC tokens become non-redeemable for that specific USDC version. The USDC sits in the contract with no withdrawal path since withdraw reverts on whitelist check.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: There is no dead-end state recovery: once de-whitelisted, the whitelist check in withdraw blocks all redemptions for that version. The usdcBalances mapping still holds the amount, but it's inaccessible. The only partial recovery is if the owner re-whitelists.

proof_of_signal.pattern: withdraw requires whitelistedUSDCVersions[_usdcVersion] == true; updateWhitelist can set to false with no check on outstanding balances

preconditions: Owner de-whitelists a USDC version that has non-zero usdcBalances
Users have deposited that USDC version and hold asdUSDC

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: updateWhitelist(version, false) does not check usdcBalances[version] > 0; withdraw requires whitelist check before allowing redemption
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Owner de-whitelists version (e.g., during migration). All depositors of that version cannot withdraw. asdUSDC tokens are burned but USDC is not returned.
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Locked USDC becomes inaccessible. Owner could extract via recover() if re-whitelisted, or USDC is permanently stuck.

economic_questions: Is there a migration path for de-whitelisted versions?
Can the owner re-whitelist to unblock withdrawals?
What total value is at risk per USDC version?
validation_requirements: Test: whitelist version → deposit → de-whitelist → attempt withdraw
Verify withdraw reverts with 'not whitelisted' after de-whitelisting
Confirm no alternative withdrawal mechanism exists

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: All deposits of de-whitelisted version locked

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All depositors of the de-whitelisted USDC version
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: 100% of deposited USDC for that version
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Total deposits of the affected USDC version

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Check if updateWhitelist has any balance guards

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
